accurate.why: Claims the rule 'would be treated as if it had never taken effect' — CRA disapproval bars reissuance of a substantially similar rule but does not retroactively erase the rule; this overstates the legal effect.

no_hallucinations.why: The characterization of CRA disapproval as treating the rule 'as if it had never taken effect' is a legal overstatement not supported by the bill text or CRA mechanics.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies purpose as nullifying the WOTUS rule under the CRA.

changes_to_existing_law.why: States the rule shall have no force or effect.

exceptions_conditions.why: No material exceptions in the resolution.

effective_dates.why: No effective date specified beyond enactment.

correct_figures.why: Federal Register citation and date are correct.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
